title: Choosing the Right Path: University vs Vocational School
passage:
Choosing Your Path: University or Vocational School?

After finishing high school, young people must choose their next step. Some students choose to go to university. This is called higher education. Other students choose to go to a vocational school. This is called vocational training. Both choices are very good, but they are different in many ways.

University programs usually last for four years. Students at university study many subjects from books. They learn about theories and ideas. For example, they study business, history, science, or languages. University students spend a lot of time in classrooms, lecture halls, and libraries. After finishing their studies, they get a degree. Many university graduates work in offices, schools, or hospitals. Common jobs for them are teachers, doctors, office managers, and scientists.

Vocational training is different. These programs are usually shorter, lasting from six months to two years. Students at vocational schools do not study a lot of theories. Instead, they learn practical skills for a specific job. They practice with their hands and use real tools. For example, they learn how to repair cars, build houses, or cook professional food. Common jobs for vocational graduates are car mechanics, builders, electric workers, and chefs.

Cost is another big difference. University can be very expensive. Students have to pay a lot of money for tuition fees, accommodation, and books. Vocational schools are usually much cheaper because the courses are shorter. However, university graduates often earn higher salaries in the long run. On the other hand, vocational graduates can start working and earning money much faster.

In conclusion, both university and vocational training are excellent choices. Students should choose the path that fits their personal interests, money, and future job goals.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: How long do university programs usually last?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'University programs usually last for four years.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do university students study from?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Students at university study many subjects from books.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Where do vocational students practice their skills?

options.B: With their hands and real tools

questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'They practice with their hands and use real tools.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why are vocational schools usually cheaper?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'Vocational schools are usually much cheaper because the courses are shorter.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: University graduates can work as doctors or teachers.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Common jobs for them are teachers, doctors, office managers, and scientists.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Vocational training programs can last for five years.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'These programs are usually shorter, lasting from six months to two years.' Five years is incorrect.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: All vocational school students must buy expensive computers.
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that university students buy books and pay tuition, but it does not mention anything about vocational students buying expensive computers.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: University graduates often earn higher salaries in the long run.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'However, university graduates often earn higher salaries in the long run.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: After finishing high school, young people must choose their next _______.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 states: 'After finishing high school, young people must choose their next step.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Students at vocational schools learn practical skills for a _______ job.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'Instead, they learn practical skills for a specific job.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Vocational graduates can start working and _______ money much faster.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'On the other hand, vocational graduates can start working and earning money much faster.'

vocabulary.meaning: education at a college or university
training that teaches skills for a specific job or trade
ideas or principles of a subject, rather than practical experience
relating to real activities and hands-on work
money paid for instruction or classes at a school or university

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài đạt kết quả cao (A1-A2)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Ở trình độ này, các câu hỏi sử dụng từ vựng giống hệt trong bài đọc (ít paraphrase). Hãy gạch chân từ khóa trong câu hỏi (ví dụ: 'four years', 'practical skills') rồi quét nhanh bài đọc để tìm đoạn chứa từ đó.

2. Đọc kỹ yêu cầu số lượng từ: Đối với dạng điền từ (Sentence Completion), đề bài yêu cầu 'NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS' (Không quá 2 từ). Hãy chắc chắn bạn điền đúng số từ và lấy chính xác từ trong văn bản, không tự ý thay đổi dạng của từ.

3. Phân biệt False và Not Given: Nếu thông tin trong câu hỏi trái ngược hoàn toàn với bài đọc, chọn FALSE. Nếu thông tin không được nhắc tới hoặc không thể tìm thấy trong bài, chọn NOT GIVEN.
